measurement: SG per-pellet core-hit fraction inside soda-twinkling-bunny's Hit Rate ▲38.91% windows vs matched HR-off windows in the SAME fight — the pre-registered CONE_DELTA post-flip holdout
status: INTERIM — near band only, n too small to score. Not a verdict. See openItems.
priorRecon: docs/probe-data/soda-tb-control-recon.json (2026-07-15; its hit_rate_core_test was INCONCLUSIVE — this is the first actual count)

clock.caveat: single anchor — the skill's hardened rule wants >=4 anchors across the fight; game-clock drift vs video (~2s) is NOT yet corrected here

design.why: Her Hit Rate ▲38.91% (>=20 Golden Chips) is a BURST-slot effect, so every HR window sits inside a Full Burst. But the boss band script (docs/data/range-data.md) puts each of her 5 burst windows cleanly inside exactly ONE band, and the gaps between them give an HR-OFF control in the same band, same fight, same unit.
design.castTimesVerified: 2026-07-22 — read off the BURST!! splash + the Golden-Chip counter drop at 1 fps, NOT taken from the 2026-07-15 recon's approximations. Three differ: burst 3 is 82 (recon ~84), burst 4 is 123 (recon ~126, a 3 s error), burst 2 is 47 (recon 48). Bursts 1 and 5 confirmed at 10 and 162.
design.gateCheck: all 5 bursts clear the >=20-chip gate on the PRE-consume count (50/44/39/36/30 as read), so the HR buff fires on every one
design.bandScript: fightClock: mid 0-33, near 33-70, far 70-106, midfar 106-144, near 144-176, midfar 176-180

countingRule.core: RED numerals with 'CORE HIT' above (crit+core = red + crit icon)
countingRule.denominator: every distinguishable popup numeral in the crosshair region of the sampled frame
countingRule.sampling: 1 frame every 1.2 s (popup persistence ~0.4-0.5 s, so no popup is counted twice)
countingRule.excluded: red VFX blooms with no numerals, the red low-ammo counter box, red laser sights, blue/cyan shield hits, the FB countdown

ownerCount.countedBy: owner, by hand, 2026-07-22 — the count of record. Supersedes the partial agent-sampled read below (kept for provenance only).
ownerCount.format: per shot: [landed pellet popups, of which RED 'CORE HIT']
ownerCount.windowsCounted: priority ranks 1-4 (all four bands, ON + OFF). Ranks 5 (extra near n) not counted.
ownerCount.ownerObservation: NO PELLET EVER LEFT HER GREY HIT CIRCLE — every pellet that missed the boss missed INSIDE the drawn accuracy circle. (Owner's sentence was truncated mid-thought; the consequence they were drawing has been asked for, not assumed.) Direct consequence either way: pellets flying outside the drawn circle is NOT a loss channel, so landing = the boss body's coverage of the AIM CIRCLE — which is exactly the basis step 3's BAND_SG_HIT_FRAC rebuild is meant to move to.

verdicts.preRegisteredHoldout: near ON measured 0.074 vs cone 0.160 -> miss 0.087, INSIDE the +-0.12 tolerance. The holdout does NOT fire its STOP trigger. But it misses in a consistent direction, and near ON lands on the cone's HR0 value (0.073) and the fallback's ON value (0.070).
verdicts.coneCoreOverPrediction: the cone over-predicts core in 3 of 4 bands ON (near 0.074 vs 0.160; mid 0.029 vs 0.133; far 0.022 vs 0.051) and is near-exact at midfar (0.079 vs 0.077). Pooled ON 0.050 vs 0.108 expected (~2.2x over). Pooled OFF 0.008 vs 0.049 expected: 1 core observed where ~6.4 were expected, P(<=1) ~ 0.011.
verdicts.fallbackUnderPrediction: the CONE_DELTA=0 fallback predicts pooled ON 0.019 vs measured 0.050 (~2.7x under), and predicts exactly ZERO at mid/far where cores were observed (5 and 3). Neither model is right; measured sits between them.
verdicts.hrDoesRaiseCore: CONFIRMED in direction: pooled ON 0.050 vs OFF 0.008, fisher p = 0.029. The magnitude of the lift is larger than the cone's ~2.2x, but the OFF arm is only 130 pellets.
verdicts.bandOrderingInversion: midfar (0.079) > mid (0.029) in the ON arm — the registered ordering near>mid>midfar>far is VIOLATED in the data at fisher p = 0.068. NOT significant at 0.05, so this is a flag, not a fired trigger. It is strange on its face: at midfar FEWER pellets land (0.778 vs 0.916) yet MORE of them core.
verdicts.landingIsHrDependent: the live engine models SG landing as a fixed per-band table with NO Hit-Rate term. Measured lift is +0.108 pooled (p = 0.0014), +0.151 at near (p = 0.006), +0.141 at mid (p = 0.021); midfar/far lifts are positive but not individually significant. This is a real modeling gap in the shipped engine.
verdicts.landingShapeVsModels: measured HR0 landing (near 0.780 / mid 0.775 / midfar 0.700 / far 0.640) contradicts ALL four candidates: the live table is too high and mis-shaped (it says mid 0.986 > near 0.888; measured says near ~ mid), geoStatic matches at near (0.797 vs 0.780) but is too low elsewhere, geoGauss is too high everywhere, and the drake popupcount table (0.60/0.60/0.55/0.45) is ~0.15-0.19 too low in every band.
verdicts.geoGaussShapeWrong: the Gaussian sigma-shrink form gets the DIRECTION of the HR lift right and the SHAPE wrong: it predicts the largest lift at far (+0.196) and the smallest at near (+0.076); measured is the reverse (near +0.151, far +0.071).
verdicts.drakeReconciliation: HYPOTHESIS, testable: the drake popupcount landing (0.60 near) was measured by pooling popups from frames sampled at <=2 fps, which systematically UNDERCOUNTS pellets per shot because popup persistence (~0.45 s) is shorter than the sampling interval. This owner count is per-shot and reads 0.78 at the same band on the same boss with no Hit Rate. If that is the explanation, SGLANDING=popupcount is biased low and the live table biased high, with the truth between them.

ownerRulingOnModelForm.ruling: 2026-07-22 — no pellet ever leaves the drawn grey hit circle, which establishes that the AIM CIRCLE + BAND_SG_HIT_FRAC formulation is the ACCURATE MODEL FORM for SG landing. The current hit-fraction VALUES may not be exact, but they are 'as close as we're probably getting'.
ownerRulingOnModelForm.whatThisSettles: the FORM question inside step 3 — landing is the boss body's coverage of the aim circle, not a spread disc pellets can escape. It is not a licence to freeze the values: see the measured-vs-table gap below.
ownerRulingOnModelForm.measuredVsCurrentTable: the owner's own HR-off count is a direct measurement of that same quantity and matches BAND_SG_HIT_FRAC at near (0.780 vs 0.797) but not elsewhere: mid 0.775 vs 0.710, midfar 0.700 vs 0.634, far 0.640 vs 0.465 (a 0.175 gap, 38% relative). So the form is validated and the values are measurably improvable, far most of all — worth re-raising before step 3 freezes them.

vsTheSim.premise: VERIFIED — soda-twinkling-bunny's Hit Rate ▲38.91 is UNMODELED (her override mentions it in prose only, no hitRatePct block; none of little-mermaid / crown / helm grants Hit Rate either). So the sim runs her at hr=0 for the whole fight and has NO ON/OFF arms: one core value and one landing value per band, applied to every shot.
vsTheSim.hrOnShareOfHerShots: 45% — her HR is on 75 s (5 x 15 s), helm's full bursts cover ~70 s more, leaving ~35 s of out-of-full-burst gaps (~5-6 s each, matching the owner's account). At ~1.33 shots/s inside a full burst and ~0.85 outside, that is ~100 HR-on shots of ~223.

core.note: measured ON / measured OFF / fight-average at the 45% split / what the sim applies
core.strength: the OFF arm is thin per band (0 cores in 28-39 pellets), but POOLED the sim expects ~6.4 cores across the 130 OFF pellets and 1 was seen: P(<=1) ~ 0.011. So the over-credit in the HR-off state — which is 55% of her shots — is supported.

vsTheSim.compensatingErrorFinding: MODELLING HER HIT RATE FAITHFULLY WOULD MAKE THE SIM WORSE IN EVERY BAND. Fight-average measured vs sim-today vs sim-with-HR: near 0.033 / 0.073 / 0.113; mid 0.031 / 0.060 / 0.093; midfar 0.035 / 0.034 / 0.053; far 0.010 / 0.022 / 0.035. The unmodelled HR block is currently masking the cone's over-prediction. Adding it — the obviously 'faithful' fix, and an open item in her override note — would roughly double the error. This is a compensating-error pair and neither half should be touched alone.

landing.note: sim table vs measured. The ON arm is a complete census (ammo-trace verified), so the ON column alone is a lower bound on the sim's error.
landing.shapeError: ROBUST, independent of the OFF-arm counting question: the sim's table says mid (0.986) > near (0.888); the measurement says mid ~ near in both arms (ON 0.916 vs 0.931, OFF 0.775 vs 0.780). The mid cell is the worst in the table.

vsTheSim.combinedOnHerSgNormals: landing x (1 + core): sim over-credits by 8.7% near, 20.9% mid, 20.6% midfar, 11.5% far — ~15% mean. Sign and size are consistent with her sitting HOT, though note her only GRADED comp is N3 (boss Iron, reading ~1.015); the control comp this count comes from is ungraded, so that cross-check is indicative rather than direct.

shotCountReconciliation.why: The ON/OFF shot-rate gap (1.50/s vs 0.46/s) was checked against the ammo counter rather than accepted, because the landing comparison rests on the OFF arm being a complete sample. Owner's account: every window had exactly 1 reload, and the reload eats a larger share of the shorter OFF window.
shotCountReconciliation.near_ON_163.5_175.5: AMMO TRACE MATCHES THE COUNT EXACTLY. Readings 007(163.5) -> 000(167.8) = 7 shots, reload, 009(169.0) -> 000(~174.4) = 9 shots, total ~16 = the 16 shots counted. The ON arm is a complete census.
shotCountReconciliation.near_OFF_150.5_160.5: AMMO TRACE DOES NOT MATCH. Sampled at 2 fps: 006(150.0) 005 004 004, a reload, then an unambiguous 009(153.0) 008 007 006 006 005 004 003 003 002 001 000(158.5) — that stretch ALONE is 9 shots in 5.5 s, plus 1-2 before the reload, so ~10-11 shots in the window against 5 counted. The window also contains TWO reloads (~152 and ~159), not one.
shotCountReconciliation.consequence: The core-fraction results are essentially unaffected — cores/pellets is a ratio over counted pellets and shot selection would have to correlate with coring to bias it. The LANDING comparison is affected asymmetrically: the ON arm is complete (16/16) while the OFF arm is ~5 of ~11 shots. If the uncounted OFF shots were passed over because they landed few or no pellets (a low-landing shot renders few popups and is easy to miss), then OFF landing 0.780 is an UPPER bound and the true HR lift is LARGER than +0.151, not smaller. The finding's direction survives either way; its magnitude is a lower bound pending reconciliation.
shotCountReconciliation.status: OPEN — needs the owner to reconcile (different sub-window? shots deliberately skipped as unreadable?) before the landing magnitudes are used for anything.

fullBurstWindowsMeasured.why: The FB-extension ladder and the sim's rotation phase both hinge on the real Full Burst durations and gaps. Measured 2026-07-22 by reading the on-screen FB countdown directly (it ticks 1.00/s — verified across 8 consecutive reads), NOT from the 2026-07-15 recon's approximate start times.

derived.fb_helm_cast142: 7.43 left at 149.5 → ends 156.93; a 15.0s duration puts its start at 141.93, matching helm's cast
derived.fb_soda_cast162: 13.29 left at 164.2 → ends 177.49; 3.61 at 173.8 → ends 177.41. DURATION EXACTLY 15.0s (start 162.49 vs her chip-verified 162 cast)
derived.fb10: EXISTS — 10.28 left at 182.5, ends 192.78. A 15.0s duration would need a 177.78 start, impossible while FB9 ran to 177.5. A 12.0s duration puts the start at 180.78, and the chip trace reads 19 chips at t=180.

conclusions: The +5s tier is DIRECTLY CONFIRMED: a Full Burst measured at exactly 15.0s while she held ≥20 chips. The old flat +4 (14.0s) is REFUTED by this — a 14.0s window cannot display 14.71 (the recon's own peak), let alone 13.29 remaining 2.2s after its start.
The +2s tier is DIRECTLY CONFIRMED TOO: the 10th Full Burst runs ~12.0s and the chip counter reads 19 at that moment — below the ≥20 threshold, exactly where the ladder predicts the drop.

CORRECTION of an agent claim made earlier this session: 'reality's inter-FB gap is ~3-5s' was WRONG. It was inferred from the recon's approximate start times. Measured, the FB9→FB10 chain gap is 5.56s, matching the owner's read of the video. Gaps VARY (~3-6s across the fight); the sim's 4.3-7.0s spread is not out of line, and the 'sim's gap is 1.2s too long' diagnosis does not survive.

Real Full Burst count is 10, and the sim on the fbext branch produces 9. The shortfall is NOT the gap: reality's 10th Full Burst is short (12s) BECAUSE her chip pool had drained below 20 by t=180, whereas the sim still held 28 pre-consume at her last cast (165.88). The open question is the CHIP TRAJECTORY late-fight — sim rebuild too fast, or her cast cadence drifting — not the inter-FB gap.

near_HR0_OFF.note: immediately BEFORE her 5th burst — same band, same fight phase; partly inside helm's Full Burst, which matches FB state without granting her Hit Rate

modelComparison.note: predictions recomputed from src/engine/sg-geometry.ts at the shipped frozen params
modelComparison.interimRead: ON 0.061 misses the cone's 0.160 by 0.099 — INSIDE the +-0.12 tolerance, so the holdout does NOT fire its STOP trigger on this count — and sits 0.009 from the fallback's 0.070. The 95% CI [0.021, 0.165] covers BOTH models, so this does not discriminate them yet.

confounds: DENOMINATOR UNCERTAINTY is the dominant error: popups overlap into stacks (the same value printed 5-7x on top of itself). Counts of distinguishable instances carry roughly +-30%; at 3/40 the fraction is 0.075, at 3/60 it is 0.050.

Value attribution is NOT clean: several popups in the ON window exceed the per-pellet band (e.g. 1029147, 1836099) and are plausibly her skill/rider hits, not pellets. They inflate the denominator; the numerator (cores) is unaffected unless a skill hit itself cored.

The ON window is inside her own Full Burst by construction (the HR buff is burst-gated), so ON frames carry heavy gold VFX while some OFF frames do not. If gold VFX hides red numerals, ON is biased LOW — i.e. the direction that would make the cone look worse. Not quantified.
The in-combat damage counter is TEAM TOTAL, so the per-shot lattice method (the SG gold standard on solo footage) is unavailable here — popup counting is the only route on this recording.
observations: Buff-state value tiers move fast in the ON window (white 218079 -> 229583 -> 218495) and the crit/plain ratio reads 1.373-1.377 in FB but 1.483-1.878 outside it — worth a separate look, not chased here.

One clean core popup gave core/plain = 357129/229583 = 1.5556 while crit/plain = 316027/229583 = 1.3765 in the same frame. Under a purely additive major bracket with base M those imply inconsistent M (1.328 from crit, ~0.74 core bonus from core). Recorded as an OBSERVATION against the 2026-07-22 additive crit/core ruling — single frame, values partly overlapped, NOT a challenge to it.

countWindows.note: Owner is counting these by hand (2026-07-22). Video seconds. Every window is trimmed 1.5 s inside its boundaries so the burst cut-in, the buff expiry, and the 1 s unhittable gap at each band breakpoint all fall outside. Band edges: mid 5-38, near 38-75, far 75-111, midfar 111-149, near 149-181 (fightClock = video - 5).
countWindows.recordPerShot: for each shot (one ammo-counter decrement): how many damage popups it produced (= landed pellets) and how many of those were RED with 'CORE HIT'. Per-window totals + shot count are an acceptable fallback.
countWindows.blind: Predicted values are deliberately NOT restated here next to the windows — they are in modelComparison above and in the 2026-07-22 handoff. Counting first and comparing after keeps the count blind.

stepThreeCoupling.finding: These same frames carry the SG LANDING measurement, which is the rebuild target of the engine plan's step 3 (BAND_SG_HIT_FRAC on the aim circle). One popup = one landed pellet is established (sg-pellet-landing.json part 1: no value-merging, off-boss pellets render nothing, dropout factor 1.0 [1,1.3]), so popups per shot / 10 IS the landing fraction. The marginal cost is reading the ammo counter, which is in frame in every sample already taken.
stepThreeCoupling.whyItMatters: Four candidate near-band landing numbers disagree by ~50%: live table 0.888, geo static BAND_SG_HIT_FRAC 0.797, pelletLandFrac Gaussian 0.922, and the only direct measurement (drake popupcount) 0.600. Dropout cannot reconcile 0.600 with 0.888 (0.600 x 1.3 = 0.78). The live table was calibrated against the OLD flat-0.85 core model (stated in sim.ts); the cone replaced the core half in 2026-07-19 and the landing half was never re-derived.
stepThreeCoupling.uniqueToThisFootage: her ▲38.91 windows make this the only SG recording with a Hit-Rate toggle inside ONE fight, so it tests whether landing is HR-dependent at all — live table says no; the Gaussian form predicts near 0.922 -> 0.998 (+8% relative).
stepThreeCoupling.byProductEstimate: NOT RELIABLE, recorded only to show the signal is there: popups per sampled frame averaged ~7.2 at near in both arms (ON 10/6/5/8/7/7, OFF 9/5/9/6). Shot cadence from the ammo counter is a solid 0.6 s/shot in both arms. But popup persistence (~0.4-0.5 s) aliases against that cadence, and reload-gap frames were excluded, so popups-per-FRAME is a biased estimator of pellets-per-SHOT in both directions. A real number needs per-shot 60 fps stepping (the coreband2 method), not this sampling.
stepThreeCoupling.guardrail: Per-unit SG landing is CLOSED (owner override 2026-07-17, open-questions A31 (U17): class table stands, per-unit refit REJECTED). Any landing number from this footage is a CLASS-table / band-shape check and a step-3 geometry target — never a per-unit value for soda-twinkling-bunny.

openItems: PROTOCOL UPGRADE: re-run as a JOINT pass — per band, step 60 fps across individual shots and record (a) popups per shot = landed pellets (step 3) and (b) cores among them (step 1). Same frames, both deliverables, and it fixes the denominator problem that limits the core count.
Extend the near-band count to n>=100 per arm (roughly 12 more frames per arm) before scoring.
Count the other three bands: mid (burst 1, video 12-27), far (burst 3, 84-99), midfar (burst 4, 126-141), each against its adjacent HR-off gap.
Re-anchor the clock on >=4 timer reads across the fight (the hardened rule) before the band edges are trusted to the second.
Second recording available for replication: docs/probes/720-kit-audit/soda tb.MP4 (2026-07-21, liter/crown/soda-twinkling-bunny/helm).
